jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Car crash sound effects and beeping
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation after yet another driving mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase perfectly integrated with physical comedy setup

jokes.text: Oh, Doctor, I'm crazy. [Sobbing]
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically declaring himself crazy
jokes.punchline: The overwrought emotional delivery of such a simple self-diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer overreaction, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Bill. Bill. Ooh. 'Llib.' Wait a minute. Bill. Huh?
jokes.setup: Homer looking through mail
jokes.punchline: Homer being confused by his own name spelled backwards, then solving it
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's mind working in mysterious ways

jokes.text: Finally, a chance to relive my golden college years. -Dad, you only took one course.
jokes.setup: Homer enthusiastically talking about reliving college
jokes.punchline: Lisa's factual correction that he barely attended
jokes.explanation: Perfect father-daughter dynamic with Lisa as reality-check straight man

jokes.text: Remember my love affair with Ali MacGraw? She used to call me 'Preppie.' Then she died.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming a romance with famous actress Ali MacGraw
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact delivery that she died (she didn't)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd non-sequitur with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: I hate Springfield U so much. - You went to Springfield U. You hate A&M. - So much.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing hatred for his own school
jokes.punchline: Lisa correcting him, Homer agreeing enthusiastically to hate the right team
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer confusion followed by unwavering commitment to whatever Lisa says

jokes.text: Let's matriculate. [Giggling]
jokes.setup: Homer using college terminology
jokes.punchline: His suggestive tone implying sexual meaning to an innocent academic word
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding academic terms as sexual innuendo

jokes.text: You college boys are only interested in one thing.
jokes.setup: Marge's accusation about college boys' singular focus
jokes.punchline: The implication that it's sex, setting up the next joke
jokes.explanation: Standard setup line that works mainly to feed the upcoming punchlines

jokes.text: Or a ski hat if you've got a picture of a girl.
jokes.setup: Previous line about hanging necktie for actual girl in room
jokes.punchline: The pathetic escalation that even a picture merits door signage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke that perfectly captures nerd desperation with unexpected specificity

jokes.text: Oh, my, yes. I'm on a secret project that I'm not at liberty to divulge. Cyborgs.
jokes.setup: Claiming secrecy about a project
jokes.punchline: Immediately blurting out exactly what it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy in the contradiction between secrecy and immediate disclosure

jokes.text: I invented a program that downloads porn off the Internet one million times faster.
jokes.setup: Nerd proudly describing his invention
jokes.punchline: It's for pornography, delivered with scientific pride
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing nerd applying technical skills to base desires

jokes.text: Does anybody need that much porno? Oh. One million times.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning the necessity
jokes.punchline: Homer suddenly understanding and being impressed by the magnitude
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment showing his thought process and shifting priorities

jokes.text: Yeah. So because of me, all the dorms now have security phones.
jokes.setup: Nerd explaining a campus security improvement
jokes.punchline: Implying he caused whatever incident necessitated security phones
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny implication that this nerdy character has a concerning past

jokes.text: In home country, growing up in filth, Lubchenko dreams to play U.S.A. football.
jokes.setup: Foreign athlete giving traditional immigrant success speech
jokes.punchline: The overly dramatic 'growing up in filth' description
jokes.explanation: Solid satire of overwrought immigrant athlete stories

jokes.text: Long story short. Go, Springfield U!
jokes.setup: Athlete starting emotional backstory speech
jokes.punchline: Dean cutting him off abruptly for fundraising purposes
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic interruption showing priorities of fundraising over human interest

jokes.text: Seal the exits.
jokes.setup: Dean announcing this is a fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Immediately trapping people like a hostage situation
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that perfectly captures aggressive fundraising tactics with prison-like efficiency

jokes.text: Get every dime. The Whifflesnops need new cummerbunds. #We look like slobs #
jokes.setup: Aggressive money collection
jokes.punchline: It's for a ridiculous group name needing formal wear accessories
jokes.explanation: Absurdist group name 'Whifflesnops' combined with formal wear desperation creates great contrast

jokes.text: I'm beginning to think this alumni party was just a ruse to get our money.
jokes.setup: After being literally trapped and robbed
jokes.punchline: Homer's mild suspicion about what's obviously a scam
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - stating the obvious long after it's obvious to everyone else

jokes.text: That dean is gonna get an indignant e-mail. You should do it with bold red letters. My computer has 512 shades of red.
jokes.setup: Threatening email revenge
jokes.punchline: The nerdy technical specifications and color options for angry email
jokes.explanation: Great nerd characterization - turning even revenge into technical specifications

jokes.text: Have you nerds forgotten everything I taught you? This calls for a prank. I see... a bucket.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing high-tech solutions
jokes.punchline: His primitive, physical prank solution with dramatic bucket identification
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between nerd solutions and Homer's analog approach, dramatic bucket spotting

jokes.text: I think I know that guy. He ran over the dean five years ago. Punch up that picture.
jokes.setup: Looking at security footage
jokes.punchline: Casually identifying someone by their vehicular homicide history
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in casually referencing past dean-running-over incident

jokes.text: Good work. Now let's see who's having sex in the library. Aw, they scrambled it.
jokes.setup: Using high-tech surveillance effectively
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to voyeuristic purposes, thwarted by scrambling
jokes.explanation: Good nerd characterization showing immediate shift to perverted uses of technology

jokes.text: Oh, real original. Who did this? - Kappa Gamma Tau. - Last in grades, first in pranks.
jokes.setup: Dean sarcastically commenting on prank originality
jokes.punchline: The fraternity's proud motto about prioritizing pranks over academics
jokes.explanation: Good college humor about misplaced priorities, well-structured motto

jokes.text: Oh, that's it. You're through, Dean. Now who's the dean?
jokes.setup: Dean getting angry at being pranked
jokes.punchline: His illogical declaration that he's firing himself and asking who's dean
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic of self-firing creating immediate confusion about succession

jokes.text: I tried greasing the bucket with bacon fat, but your father kept eating it.
jokes.setup: Trying to remove bucket from Homer's head
jokes.punchline: Homer's inability to resist eating the lubricant they're trying to use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - his food obsession sabotaging practical solutions

jokes.text: Well, couldn't you try a nondelicious fat? [Sobbing] Oh, there's no such thing.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting alternative lubricant
jokes.punchline: Homer's emotional breakdown over the impossibility of non-tasty fats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic and emotional investment in the deliciousness of all fats

jokes.text: Beneath that bucket, he's more glue than man.
jokes.setup: Medical assessment of Homer's condition
jokes.punchline: The dramatic declaration of his transformation into glue-being
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic medical pronouncement with poetic absurdity

jokes.text: These days the victims of comedy traumas, or 'traumadies'
jokes.setup: Doctor discussing comedy-related injuries
jokes.punchline: The portmanteau word 'traumadies' for comedy-caused trauma
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that acknowledges the show's own physical comedy conventions

jokes.text: What guys? I want to see the freaks. Okay, hang on, Homer. Two eye holes coming up. Easy. Easy. Too far.
jokes.setup: Homer with bucket on head wanting to see something
jokes.punchline: Bart drilling eye holes and going too far with the drill
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy with implied visual of drilling mishap

jokes.text: [Homer] That had nothing to do with the bucket.
jokes.setup: After car crash sounds while Homer's driving with bucket
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive clarification that his poor driving wasn't bucket-related
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer defensive logic and misdirection of blame

jokes.text: Wow. He dances better than Jesus himself.
jokes.setup: Watching Brother Faith perform
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual comparison of dancing skills with Jesus
jokes.explanation: Great irreverent Homer observation treating religious figure as dance competitor

jokes.text: I done 'spraint' my elbow bone... so it goes in the 'opposited' direction.
jokes.setup: Hillbilly explaining his injury
jokes.punchline: The mispronunciations 'spraint' and 'opposited' while describing backwards elbow
jokes.explanation: Good Cletus characterization with creative mispronunciations and absurd injury

jokes.text: He paralyzed his vocal cords cramming too many 'K' sounds into a punch line.
jokes.setup: Krusty can't speak properly
jokes.punchline: Homer's diagnosis of comedy-induced injury from alliteration overuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about comedy writing techniques causing physical harm to performers

jokes.text: Have you gone completely fakakta? Hey, I got my comedy K's back. King Kong coldcocked Kato Kaelin.
jokes.setup: Krusty being healed of his K-sound problem
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating recovery with excessive K-alliteration
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback demonstrating the K-sound recovery with period-specific pop culture reference

jokes.text: Hey, you gentiles are all right. [Kisses]
jokes.setup: Krusty being grateful for healing
jokes.punchline: The Jewish clown's backhanded compliment to non-Jews
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty character moment with religious/cultural humor

jokes.text: Brother, I sense you are feeling trapped and desperate. Yeah. And I got a bucket on my head.
jokes.setup: Faith's spiritual diagnosis of emotional state
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation focusing on physical bucket problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection between spiritual and literal interpretation of problems

jokes.text: Son, lay hands upon your father's burdensome bucket. Now proclaim, 'I have the power.'
jokes.setup: Religious healing ritual setup
jokes.punchline: The He-Man reference mixed with faith healing ceremony
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference integration into religious context

jokes.text: I... have... the power. I have the power!
jokes.setup: Bart reluctantly saying the phrase
jokes.punchline: His growing confidence and enthusiasm in the delivery
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Bart getting caught up in the moment

jokes.text: I see the light. It burns.
jokes.setup: Traditional religious awakening phrase
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal complaint about actual bright lights hurting his eyes
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from spiritual to literal interpretation of 'seeing the light'

jokes.text: I believe I had hair. Oh, here they are. [Grunts]
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy thinking he's been healed of baldness
jokes.punchline: Finding his hair was just displaced/hidden, not regrown
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy deflating miracle expectations

jokes.text: Really? Hmm. I would think he would want to limit my power.
jokes.setup: Being told God gave him healing power
jokes.punchline: Bart's self-aware acknowledgment of his troublemaking nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart self-awareness about his reputation and God's likely concerns

jokes.text: I figure I'll go for the life of sin... followed by the presto-change-o deathbed repentance.
jokes.setup: Discussing life philosophy with Brother Faith
jokes.punchline: Bart's calculated plan to sin then conveniently repent at the end
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart logic gaming the religious system with childlike calculation

jokes.text: Wow. That's a good angle.
jokes.setup: Bart's deathbed repentance plan
jokes.punchline: The preacher's professional appreciation of the theological loophole
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing the preacher's business-like appreciation of religious strategy

jokes.text: Devil, be gone. Ow! My milk money. And my milk. Ew!
jokes.setup: Bart attempting to heal Ralph's breathing problem
jokes.punchline: The healing 'works' by making Ralph vomit up money and milk
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out comedy with Ralph's typical bizarre internal logic

jokes.text: Number of miracles performed by Bart, two. Number performed by Lisa, zero.
jokes.setup: Lisa being skeptical of Bart's powers
jokes.punchline: Her scientific scorekeeping and competitive frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa characterization - applying logic while still being competitive with Bart

jokes.text: Heat makes metal expand. Now who's talking mumbo jumbo?
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining the bucket removal scientifically
jokes.punchline: Bart sarcastically calling basic physics 'mumbo jumbo'
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal with Bart dismissing science and Lisa's rational explanations

jokes.text: Look at Moe dance. #Aloha, Moe #
jokes.setup: Homer's homecoming float concept
jokes.punchline: Completely random Moe-dancing theme with Hawaiian greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Homer creativity with inexplicable Moe focus and Hawaiian theme

jokes.text: I got two words for this sermon. [Snores, Whistles]
jokes.setup: Homer announcing he has two words
jokes.punchline: The 'words' are sleep sounds instead of actual criticism
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with Homer's unconscious criticism being more effective than words

jokes.text: No, really. It can be a crazy party with clouds and lasers and miracles. And chili fries.
jokes.setup: Bart defending that church can be fun
jokes.punchline: The progression from spiritual elements to snack food
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from cosmic to mundane with Bart's practical priorities showing

jokes.text: Can't make a float without flowers. Oh, true enough. But, uh, did you have to salt the earth so nothing would ever grow again?
jokes.setup: Homer taking Ned's flowers for his float
jokes.punchline: The extreme overkill of salting the earth like ancient warfare
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation to biblical/historical destruction methods for gardening spite

jokes.text: [Chuckles] Yeah.
jokes.setup: Ned's horrified question about salting the earth
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual, proud confirmation of the environmental destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - casual pride in absurd overkill destruction

jokes.text: Satan, eat my shorts.
jokes.setup: Bart starting his revival show
jokes.punchline: Combining his signature catchphrase with religious confrontation
jokes.explanation: Perfect integration of Bart's signature phrase into religious context

jokes.text: Perhaps it's time to fight razzle with dazzle.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy seeing Bart's flashy revival success
jokes.punchline: The alliterative plan to compete with showmanship
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Lovejoy's competitive response to Bart's success

jokes.text: Is he killing that guitar, Daddy? Yes, Son.
jokes.setup: Child asking about Lovejoy's terrible guitar playing
jokes.punchline: Father's matter-of-fact confirmation that it's musical murder
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan confirmation of musical incompetence with perfect economy

jokes.text: Bury me at Make Out Creek. [Groans]
jokes.setup: Milhouse injured and making dying declaration
jokes.punchline: Wants to be buried at romantic location despite never having been there with anyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse romantic delusion even in extremis, good character consistency

jokes.text: Oh, Milhouse, this is my fault. I'm so sorry.
jokes.setup: Milhouse getting hit by car after Bart 'healed' his vision
jokes.punchline: Bart's genuine remorse showing his conscience beneath the showmanship
jokes.explanation: Character development moment showing Bart's moral growth and consequences

jokes.text: And I tried to scratch and the fork got stuck in there. And I think there was some food on the fork.
jokes.setup: Milhouse describing his itchy cast problem
jokes.punchline: The escalating grossness of food contamination in his cast
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out escalation with Milhouse's typically unfortunate circumstances

jokes.text: Th-This is writing? I'm sorry, Uncle Kent. I lost my thesaurus.
jokes.setup: Kent criticizing repetitive 'fever' usage
jokes.punchline: Intern's literal interpretation that thesaurus loss caused bad writing
jokes.explanation: Good media satire showing literal-minded intern and Kent's standards

jokes.text: Oh, you're only calling us a cow college 'cause we were founded by a cow.
jokes.setup: Defending A&M against 'cow college' insult
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact admission that a cow actually founded the school
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that makes the insult literally accurate and somehow less insulting

jokes.text: Well, I think the veal might have died of loneliness.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for non-brutally slaughtered food
jokes.punchline: Marge's darkly innocent suggestion that emotional death is better
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Marge logic that's somehow more disturbing than physical violence

jokes.text: Oh, Land O'Lakes, take that ozone layer.
jokes.setup: Football announcer's colorful commentary
jokes.punchline: Random dairy product reference mixed with environmental destruction
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist sports announcer speak mixing brand names with unrelated exclamations

jokes.text: Whoa! Anna, get your radar gun, 'cause that tamale was one tall drink of water.
jokes.setup: Sports announcer describing a play
jokes.punchline: Mixing food references with liquid measurements in nonsensical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Creative announcer nonsense mixing unrelated metaphors in typical sports hyperbole

jokes.text: Oh, you just know what everyone should do, don't you, Marge?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Lloyd's of London insurance for the kicker's foot
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic, defensive response to reasonable suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good Homer defensiveness showing his insecurity about Marge's practical intelligence

jokes.text: If they don't have tabbouleh, what's your second choice? They'll have tabbouleh.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about food backup plan
jokes.punchline: Lisa's confident assumption about Middle Eastern food at football concessions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's optimistic expectations about food diversity

jokes.text: So I guess I'll have to see someone else about my crippling depression. Arr. Arr.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain hearing Bart doesn't do healing anymore
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact mention of serious mental health issues with pirate sounds
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing serious mental health with pirate character quirks

jokes.text: Wait! Wait! I got a float too. Listen up, 'cause I gotta explain a lot of it.
jokes.setup: Homer interrupting halftime with his late float
jokes.punchline: His admission that his concept requires extensive explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer self-awareness that his creative vision isn't immediately comprehensible

jokes.text: Oh. Dad has the worst luck when he's drinking.
jokes.setup: Homer causing major accident with his float
jokes.punchline: Lisa's casual correlation of drinking with Homer's misfortune
jokes.explanation: Dark family dysfunction humor with Lisa's matter-of-fact observation about alcoholism

jokes.text: But you can always fall back on your degree in... Communications? Oh, dear Lord. I know. Is phony major. [Sobbing] Lubchenko learn nothing. Nothing.
jokes.setup: Doctor mentioning Lubchenko's backup degree
jokes.punchline: The mutual recognition that Communications is worthless and Lubchenko's emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Excellent academic satire with foreign student's honest assessment of degree mill education

jokes.text: Oh, jumpin' crawdaddies! Is that Lubchenko coming back on the field?
jokes.setup: Dramatic sports announcement
jokes.punchline: The folksy exclamation 'jumpin' crawdaddies' for dramatic sports moment
jokes.explanation: Good announcer characterization with colorful Southern expressions

jokes.text: I did it! I did it! My leg, it broke off.
jokes.setup: Lubchenko celebrating his successful kick
jokes.punchline: The cheerful announcement that his leg detached during the play
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy with Lubchenko's positive attitude about dismemberment

jokes.text: I did it! I did it! My leg, it broke off.
jokes.setup: Lubchenko celebrating successful field goal
jokes.punchline: Joy mixed with realization his leg detached during the kick
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy mixing triumph with bodily harm, good dramatic irony

jokes.text: You know, drink beer out of it and so on. But it'll turn up in the morning, and I'll sew it back on. Will that really work? Well, I assume so.
jokes.setup: Explaining what fans will do with the detached leg
jokes.punchline: The casual medical uncertainty about reattaching a leg used as a beer mug
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd medical negligence combined with college party culture

jokes.text: I don't have any special powers. I am not a healer. Fine. More money for me.
jokes.setup: Bart finally admitting he has no healing powers
jokes.punchline: Doctor's immediate focus on personal financial benefit from the confession
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing doctor's mercenary priorities over patient care
